                    [32m'from reflex_base.utils.streaming_response import DisconnectAwareStreamingResponse\n'[39m +
                    [32m'from starlette.datastructures import Headers\n'[39m +
                    [32m'from starlette.datastructures import UploadFile as StarletteUploadFile\n'[39m +
                    [32m'from starlette.exceptions import HTTPException\n'[39m +
                    [32m'from starlette.formparsers import MultiPartException, _user_safe_decode\n'[39m +

                    [32m'@dataclasses.dataclass(frozen=True)\n'[39m +
                    [32m'class UploadFile(StarletteUploadFile):\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    """A file uploaded to the server.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    Args:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        file: The standard Python file object (non-async).\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        filename: The original file name.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        size: The size of the file in bytes.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        headers: The headers of the request.\n'[39m +

                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    file: BinaryIO\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    path: Path | None = dataclasses.field(default=None)\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    size: int | None = dataclasses.field(default=None)\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    headers: Headers = dataclasses.field(default_factory=Headers)\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    @property\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    def filename(self) -> str | None:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        """Get the name of the uploaded file.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        Returns:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            The name of the uploaded file.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        """\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        return self.name\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    @property\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    def name(self) -> str | None:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        """Get the name of the uploaded file.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        Returns:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            The name of the uploaded file.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        """\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        if self.path:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            return self.path.name\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        return None\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'@dataclasses.dataclass(frozen=True, kw_only=True, slots=True)\n'[39m +
                    [32m'class UploadChunk:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    """A chunk of uploaded file data."""\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    filename: str\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    offset: int\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    content_type: str\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    data: bytes\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'class UploadChunkIterator(AsyncIterator[UploadChunk]):\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    """An async iterator over uploaded file chunks."""\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    __slots__ = (\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        "_chunks",\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        "_closed",\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        "_condition",\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        "_consumer_task",\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        "_error",\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        "_maxsize",\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    )\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    def __init__(self, *, maxsize: int = 8):\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        """Initialize the iterator.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        Args:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            maxsize: Maximum number of chunks to buffer before blocking producers.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        """\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        self._maxsize = maxsize\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        self._chunks: deque[UploadChunk] = deque()\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        self._condition = asyncio.Condition()\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        self._closed = False\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        self._error: Exception | None = None\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        self._consumer_task: asyncio.Future[Any] | None = None\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    def __aiter__(self) -> Self:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        """Return the iterator itself.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        Returns:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            The upload chunk iterator.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        """\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        return self\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    async def __anext__(self) -> UploadChunk:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        """Yield the next available upload chunk.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        Returns:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            The next upload chunk.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        Raises:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            _error: Any error forwarded from the upload producer.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            StopAsyncIteration: When all chunks have been consumed.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        """\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        async with self._condition:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            while not self._chunks and not self._closed:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                await self._condition.wait()\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            if self._chunks:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                chunk = self._chunks.popleft()\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                self._condition.notify_all()\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                return chunk\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            if self._error is not None:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                raise self._error\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            raise StopAsyncIteration\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    def set_consumer_task(self, task: asyncio.Future[Any]) -> None:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        """Track the task consuming this iterator.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        Args:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            task: The background task consuming upload chunks.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        """\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        self._consumer_task = task\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        task.add_done_callback(self._wake_waiters)\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    async def push(self, chunk: UploadChunk) -> None:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        """Push a new chunk into the iterator.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        Args:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            chunk: The chunk to push.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        Raises:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            RuntimeError: If the iterator is already closed or the consumer exited early.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        """\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        async with self._condition:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            while len(self._chunks) >= self._maxsize and not self._closed:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                self._raise_if_consumer_finished()\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                await self._condition.wait()\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            if self._closed:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                msg = "Upload chunk iterator is closed."\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                raise RuntimeError(msg)\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            self._raise_if_consumer_finished()\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            self._chunks.append(chunk)\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            self._condition.notify_all()\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    async def finish(self) -> None:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        """Mark the iterator as complete."""\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        async with self._condition:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            if self._closed:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                return\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            self._closed = True\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            self._condition.notify_all()\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    async def fail(self, error: Exception) -> None:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        """Mark the iterator as failed.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        Args:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            error: The error to raise from the iterator.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        """\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        async with self._condition:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            if self._closed:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                return\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            self._closed = True\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            self._error = error\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            self._condition.notify_all()\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    def _raise_if_consumer_finished(self) -> None:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        """Raise if the consumer task exited before draining the iterator.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        Raises:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            RuntimeError: If the consumer task completed before draining the iterator.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        """\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        if self._consumer_task is None or not self._consumer_task.done():\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            return\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        try:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            task_exc = self._consumer_task.exception()\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        except asyncio.CancelledError as err:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            task_exc = err\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        msg = "Upload handler returned before consuming all upload chunks."\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        if task_exc is not None:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            raise RuntimeError(msg) from task_exc\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        raise RuntimeError(msg)\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    def _wake_waiters(self, task: asyncio.Future[Any]) -> None:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        """Wake any producers or consumers blocked on the iterator condition.\n'[39m +

                    [32m'            task: The completed consumer task.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        """\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        task.get_loop().create_task(self._notify_waiters())\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    async def _notify_waiters(self) -> None:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        """Notify tasks waiting on the iterator condition."""\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        async with self._condition:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            self._condition.notify_all()\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +

                    [32m'def get_upload_files_context_var_data() -> VarData:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    """Build the VarData for vars reading the upload-files React context.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    Defined as a function (not a module-level value) because it references\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    ``UploadFilesProvider``, which is declared further down in this module.\n'[39m +

                    [32m'        hooks={\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            "const [filesById, setFilesById] = useContext(UploadFilesContext);": None,\n'[39m +

                    [32m'        app_wraps=((5, UploadFilesProvider.create()),),\n'[39m +

                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'def upload_file(id_: str | Var[str] = DEFAULT_UPLOAD_ID) -> Var:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    """Get the file upload drop trigger.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    This var is passed to the dropzone component to update the file list when a\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    drop occurs.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    Args:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        id_: The id of the upload to get the drop trigger for.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    Returns:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        A var referencing the file upload drop trigger.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    """\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    id_var = LiteralStringVar.create(id_) if not isinstance(id_, Var) else id_\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    var_name = f"""e => setFilesById(filesById => {{\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    const updatedFilesById = Object.assign({{}}, filesById);\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    updatedFilesById[{id_var!s}] = e;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    return updatedFilesById;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'  }})\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    """\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    return Var(\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        _js_expr=var_name,\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        _var_type=EventChain,\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        _var_data=VarData.merge(\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            get_upload_files_context_var_data(), id_var._get_all_var_data()\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        ),\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    )\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +

                    [32m'        _var_data=VarData.merge(\n'[39m +

                    [32m'\n'[39m +

                    [32m'_UPLOAD_FILES_CLIENT_HANDLER = "uploadFiles"\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'def _default_drop_rejected(rejected_files: ArrayVar[list[dict[str, Any]]]) -> EventSpec:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    """Event handler for showing a toast with rejected file info.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    Args:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        rejected_files: The files that were rejected.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    Returns:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        An event spec that shows a toast with the rejected file info when triggered.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    """\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    def _format_rejected_file_record(rf: ObjectVar[dict[str, Any]]) -> str:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        rf = rf.to(ObjectVar, dict[str, dict[str, Any]])\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        file = rf["file"].to(ObjectVar, dict[str, Any])\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        errors = rf["errors"].to(ArrayVar, list[dict[str, Any]])\n'[39m +
                    [32m`        return f"{file['path']}: {errors.foreach(lambda kv: kv['message']).join(', ')}"  # noqa: FURB118\n`[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    return toast.error(\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        title="Files not Accepted",\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        description=rejected_files\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        .to(ArrayVar)\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        .foreach(_format_rejected_file_record)\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        .join("\\n\\n"),\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        close_button=True,\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        style={"white_space": "pre-line"},\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    )\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'class UploadFilesProvider(Component):\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    """AppWrap component that provides a dict of selected files by ID via useContext."""\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    library = f"$/{Dirs.CONTEXTS_PATH}"\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    tag = "UploadFilesProvider"\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +

                    [32m'    )\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    disabled: Var[bool] = field(doc="Whether the dropzone is disabled.")\n'[39m +

                    [32m'    # Marked True when any Upload component is created.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    is_used: ClassVar[bool] = False\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +

                    [32m'        doc="Fired when dropped files do not meet the specified criteria."\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    )\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +

                    [32m'        default=None,\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        is_javascript_property=False,\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    )\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    @classmethod\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    def create(cls, *children, **props) -> Component:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        """Create an upload component.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        Args:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            *children: The children of the component.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            **props: The properties of the component.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        Returns:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            The upload component.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        """\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        # Mark the Upload component as used in the app.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        cls.is_used = True\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        props.setdefault("multiple", True)\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        # Apply the default classname\n'[39m +

                    [32m'    const chunks = progressEvent.event.target.responseText.trim().split("\\n");\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    // So only process _new_ chunks beyond resp_idx.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    chunks.slice(resp_idx).map((chunk_json) => {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'      try {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        const chunk = JSON5.parse(chunk_json);\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        event_callbacks.map((f, ix) => {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'          f(chunk)\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            .then(() => {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'              if (ix === event_callbacks.length - 1) {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                // Mark this chunk as processed.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                resp_idx += 1;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'              }\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            })\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            .catch((e) => {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'              if (progressEvent.progress === 1) {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                // Chunk may be incomplete, so only report errors when full response is available.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                console.log("Error processing chunk", chunk, e);\n'[39m +
                    [32m'              }\n'[39m +
                    [32m'              return;\n'[39m +

                    [32m'          console.log("Error parsing chunk", chunk_json, e);\n'[39m +

                    [32m'        return;\n'[39m +

            [32m'   - Include AT LEAST 3-5 lines of context BEFORE the change point\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Include AT LEAST 3-5 lines of context AFTER the change point\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Include all whitespace, indentation, and surrounding code exactly as it appears in the file\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'2. EXPECTED MATCHES: If you want to replace multiple instances:\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Use the expectedReplacements parameter with the exact number of occurrences you expect to replace\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - This will replace ALL occurrences of the searchContent with the replaceContent\n'[39m +
            [32m"   - If the actual number of matches doesn't equal expectedReplacements, the edit will fail\n"[39m +
            [32m'   - This is a safety feature to prevent unintended replacements\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'3. VERIFICATION: Before using this tool:\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Check how many instances of the target text exist in the file\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - If multiple instances exist, either:\n'[39m +
            [32m'     a) Gather enough context to uniquely identify each one and make separate calls, OR\n'[39m +
            [32m'     b) Use expectedReplacements parameter with the exact count of instances you expect to replace\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'WARNING:\n'[39m +
            [32m"- The tool will fail if searchContent matches multiple locations and expectedReplacements isn't specified\n"[39m +
            [32m"- The tool will fail if the number of matches doesn't equal expectedReplacements when it's specified\n"[39m +
            [32m"- The tool will fail if searchContent doesn't match the file contents exactly (including whitespace)\n"[39m +
            [32m'- The tool will fail if searchContent and replaceContent are the same\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'When making edits:\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Ensure the edit results in idiomatic, correct code\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Do not add trailing whitespace to lines (a newline at the end of a file is fine)\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Do not leave the code in a broken state\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'If you want to create a new file, use:\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - A new file path, including dir name if needed\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - An empty searchContent\n'[39m +
            [32m"   - The new file's contents as replaceContent\n"[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'Remember: when making multiple file edits in a row to the same file, you should prefer to send all edits in a single message with multiple calls to this tool, rather than multiple messages with a single call each.\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'You may see the following fields in the result:\n'[39m +
            [32m'- autoFormattingEdits: If the auto-formatter makes any changes, this field will contain a diff against the file content after your edits and any user edits have been applied.\n'[39m +
            [32m'- newProblems: If any new problems are found after the edit, this field will contain information about them.'[39m,
          inputSchema: {
            [32m'$schema'[39m: [32m'http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#'[39m,
            type: [32m'object'[39m,
            properties: {
              path: {
                type: [32m'string'[39m,
                description: [32m'The path of the file to modify (relative to the current working directory, or an absolute path).'[39m
              },
              searchContent: {
                type: [32m'string'[39m,
                description: [32m'The text to replace.'[39m
              },
              replaceContent: {
                type: [32m'string'[39m,

        {
          type: [32m'function'[39m,
          name: [32m'executeCommand'[39m,
          description: [32m'Executes a given bash command in a persistent shell session with optional timeout, ensuring proper handling and security measures.\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'IMPORTANT: This tool is for terminal operations like git, npm, docker, etc. DO NOT use it for file operations (reading, writing, editing, searching, finding files) - use the specialized tools for this instead.\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'Before executing the command, please follow these steps:\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'1. Directory Verification:\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - If the command will create new directories or files, first use listFiles to verify the parent directory exists and is the correct location\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - For example, before running "mkdir foo/bar", first use listFiles to check that "foo" exists and is the intended parent directory\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'2. Command Execution:\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Always quote file paths that contain spaces with double quotes (e.g., cd "path with spaces/file.txt")\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Examples of proper quoting:\n'[39m +
            [32m'     - cd "/Users/name/My Documents" (correct)\n'[39m +
            [32m'     - cd /Users/name/My Documents (incorrect - will fail)\n'[39m +
            [32m'     - python "/path/with spaces/script.py" (correct)\n'[39m +
            [32m'     - python /path/with spaces/script.py (incorrect - will fail)\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - After ensuring proper quoting, execute the command.\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Capture the output of the command.\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'Usage notes:\n'[39m +
            [32m'- The command argument is required.\n'[39m +
            [32m'- You can specify an optional timeout in seconds (up to 300s / 5 minutes). If not specified, commands will timeout after 60s (1 minute).\n'[39m +
            [32m'- If the output exceeds 30000 characters, output will be truncated before being returned to you.\n'[39m +
            [32m'- When issuing multiple commands:\n'[39m +
            [32m'  - If the commands are independent and can run in parallel, make multiple executeCommand tool calls in a single message. For example, if you need to run "git status" and "git diff", send a single message with two executeCommand tool calls in parallel.\n'[39m +
            [32m'  - If the commands depend on each other and must run sequentially, use a single executeCommand call with \'&&\' to chain them together (e.g., `git add . && git commit -m "message" && git push`). For instance, if one operation must complete before another starts (like mkdir before cp, Write before executeCommand for git operations, or git add before git commit), run these operations sequentially instead.\n'[39m +
            [32m"  - Use ';' only when you need to run commands sequentially but don't care if earlier commands fail\n"[39m +
            [32m'  - DO NOT use newlines to separate commands (newlines are ok in quoted strings)\n'[39m +
            [32m"- You shall avoid use the markdown code block syntax (backtick, '`') in your command, as it will be interpreted as a command substitution.\n"[39m +
            [32m"- Before using this tool, you must first think about the context provided in <system-reminder> to understand the user's environment and tailor your commands to ensure they are compatible with their system. You must also consider if the command you need to run should be executed in a specific directory rather than the current working directory, and properly set the `cwd` if necessary.\n"[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'# Committing changes with git\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'Only create commits when requested by the user. If unclear, ask first. When the user asks you to create a new git commit, follow these steps carefully:\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'Git Safety Protocol:\n'[39m +
            [32m'- NEVER update the git config\n'[39m +
            [32m"- NEVER run destructive git commands (push --force, reset --hard, checkout ., restore ., clean -f, branch -D) unless the user explicitly requests these actions. Taking unauthorized destructive actions is unhelpful and can result in lost work, so it's best to ONLY run these commands when given direct instructions \n"[39m +
            [32m'- NEVER skip hooks (--no-verify, --no-gpg-sign, etc) unless the user explicitly requests it\n'[39m +
            [32m'- NEVER run force push to main/master, warn the user if they request it\n'[39m +
            [32m'- CRITICAL: Always create NEW commits rather than amending, unless the user explicitly requests a git amend. When a pre-commit hook fails, the commit did NOT happen  — so --amend would modify the PREVIOUS commit, which may result in destroying work or losing previous changes. Instead, after hook failure, fix the issue, re-stage, and create a NEW commit\n'[39m +
            [32m'- When staging files, prefer adding specific files by name rather than using "git add -A" or "git add .", which can accidentally include sensitive files (.env, credentials) or large binaries\n'[39m +
            [32m'- NEVER commit changes unless the user explicitly asks you to. It is VERY IMPORTANT to only commit when explicitly asked, otherwise the user will feel that you are being too proactive\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'1. You can call multiple tools in a single response. When multiple independent pieces of information are requested and all commands are likely to succeed, run multiple tool calls in parallel for optimal performance. run the following bash commands in parallel, each using the executeCommand tool:\n'[39m +
            [32m'  - Run a git status command to see all untracked files. IMPORTANT: Never use the -uall flag as it can cause memory issues on large repos.\n'[39m +
            [32m'  - Run a git diff command to see both staged and unstaged changes that will be committed.\n'[39m +
            [32m"  - Run a git log command to see recent commit messages, so that you can follow this repository's commit message style.\n"[39m +
            [32m'2. Analyze all staged changes (both previously staged and newly added) and draft a commit message:\n'[39m +
            [32m'  - Summarize the nature of the changes (eg. new feature, enhancement to an existing feature, bug fix, refactoring, test, docs, etc.). Ensure the message accurately reflects the changes and their purpose (i.e. "add" means a wholly new feature, "update" means an enhancement to an existing feature, "fix" means a bug fix, etc.).\n'[39m +
            [32m'  - Do not commit files that likely contain secrets (.env, credentials.json, etc). Warn the user if they specifically request to commit those files\n'[39m +
            [32m'  - Draft a concise (1-2 sentences) commit message that focuses on the "why" rather than the "what"\n'[39m +
            [32m'  - Ensure it accurately reflects the changes and their purpose\n'[39m +
            [32m'3. You can call multiple tools in a single response. When multiple independent pieces of information are requested and all commands are likely to succeed, run multiple tool calls in parallel for optimal performance. run the following commands:\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Add relevant untracked files to the staging area.\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Create the commit with a message ending with:\n'[39m +
            [32m'   🤖 Generated with [Pochi](https://getpochi.com) | [Task](<Share URL>)\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'   Co-Authored-By: Pochi <noreply@getpochi.com>\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'   Note: Replace <Share URL> with the Share URL from system information if available. If not available, omit the " | [Task](...)" part.\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Run git status after the commit completes to verify success.\n'[39m +
            [32m'   Note: git status depends on the commit completing, so run it sequentially after the commit.\n'[39m +
            [32m'4. If the commit fails due to pre-commit hook changes, retry the commit ONCE to include these automated changes. If it fails again, it usually means a pre-commit hook is preventing the commit. If the commit succeeds but you notice that files were modified by the pre-commit hook, you MUST amend your commit to include them.\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'Important notes:\n'[39m +
            [32m'- NEVER update the git config\n'[39m +
            [32m'- NEVER run additional commands to read or explore code, besides git bash commands\n'[39m +
            [32m'- DO NOT push to the remote repository unless the user explicitly asks you to do so\n'[39m +
            [32m'- IMPORTANT: Never use git commands with the -i flag (like git rebase -i or git add -i) since they require interactive input which is not supported.\n'[39m +
            [32m'- IMPORTANT: Do not use --no-edit with git rebase commands, as the --no-edit flag is not a valid option for git rebase.\n'[39m +
            [32m'- If there are no changes to commit (i.e., no untracked files and no modifications), do not create an empty commit\n'[39m +
            [32m'- In order to ensure good formatting, ALWAYS pass the commit message via a HEREDOC, a la this example:\n'[39m +
            [32m'<example>\n'[39m +
            [32m`git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'\n`[39m +
            [32m'Commit message here.\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'🤖 Generated with [Pochi](https://getpochi.com) | [Task](<Share URL>)\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'Co-Authored-By: Pochi <noreply@getpochi.com>\n'[39m +
            [32m'EOF\n'[39m +
            [32m')"\n'[39m +
            [32m'</example>\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'# Creating pull requests\n'[39m +
            [32m'Use the gh command via the executeCommand tool for ALL GitHub-related tasks including working with issues, pull requests, checks, and releases. If given a Github URL use the gh command to get the information needed.\n'[39m +

            [32m'   - Run a git status command to see all untracked files (never use -uall flag)\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Run a git diff command to see both staged and unstaged changes that will be committed\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Check if the current branch tracks a remote branch and is up to date with the remote, so you know if you need to push to the remote\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Run a git log command and `git diff [base-branch]...HEAD` to understand the full commit history for the current branch (from the time it diverged from the base branch)\n'[39m +

            [32m')"\n'[39m +
            [32m'</example>\n'[39m +

            [32m'Important:\n'[39m +

                [32m'## Background\n'[39m +

                [32m'Build a Reflex web application that allows end users to upload text and image files through the browser. Reflex provides an `rx.upload(...)` component along with helper utilities such as `rx.get_upload_dir()` for persisting files server-side and `rx.foreach` for rendering dynamic lists from state.\n'[39m +
                [32m'\n'[39m +
                [32m'## Requirements\n'[39m +
                [32m'\n'[39m +
                [32m'* Implement a Reflex app that renders a single page with:\n'[39m +
                [32m'  * An `rx.upload(...)` component that accepts ONLY plain text (`.txt`) and PNG image (`.png`) files.\n'[39m +
                [32m'  * A button that triggers the upload to the backend.\n'[39m +
                [32m'  * A dynamic list of every successfully uploaded filename that updates after each upload.\n'[39m +
                [32m'* The backend must read each uploaded file asynchronously, save the bytes to the Reflex upload directory using the original filename, and append that filename to the state list.\n'[39m +
                [32m'* The application must run with the standard Reflex frontend on port 3000 and the backend on port 8000.\n'[39m +
                [32m'\n'[39m +
                [32m'## Implementation Hints\n'[39m +
                [32m'\n'[39m +
                [32m'* Use `uv` to manage the Python environment as described in the Reflex setup workflow (`uv init`, `uv add reflex`, `uv run reflex init --template blank`).\n'[39m +
                [32m'* The `rx.upload` component must specify an explicit `id="upload1"` and an `accept` prop mapping the MIME types `"text/plain"` and `"image/png"` to their respective file extensions.\n'[39m +
                [32m'* Define a state class with a single base var `uploaded_files: list[str] = []`.\n'[39m +
                [32m'* The upload event handler must be an `async` method accepting `files: list[rx.UploadFile]`. For each file, await `file.read()` to read the bytes, write them to a path obtained from `rx.get_upload_dir()`, and append the filename to `uploaded_files`.\n'[39m +
                [32m'* Render the dynamic list of filenames using `rx.foreach(State.uploaded_files, ...)`.\n'[39m +
                [32m'* Bind a button\'s `on_click` to `State.handle_upload(rx.upload_files(upload_id="upload1"))` to trigger the upload.\n'[39m +
                [32m'* Make sure to kill any background processes (`reflex run`, `next`, `uvicorn`, etc.) you start before reporting the task complete.\n'[39m +
                [32m'\n'[39m +
                [32m'## Acceptance Criteria\n'[39m +

                [32m'* Frontend port: 3000\n'[39m +
                [32m'* Backend port: 8000\n'[39m +
                [32m'* The Reflex application must define a state class with a base var `uploaded_files: list[str] = []`.\n'[39m +
                [32m'* The state class must define an async event handler `handle_upload(self, files: list[rx.UploadFile])` that:\n'[39m +
                [32m"  * Reads each uploaded file's bytes via `await file.read()`.\n"[39m +
                [32m'  * Saves the bytes to the directory returned by `rx.get_upload_dir()` using the original filename.\n'[39m +
                [32m'  * Appends the original filename to `uploaded_files`.\n'[39m +
                [32m'* The compiled frontend served at http://localhost:3000 must:\n'[39m +
                [32m'  * Contain an `rx.upload` component with `id="upload1"` that accepts `text/plain` (`.txt`) and `image/png` (`.png`) files.\n'[39m +
                [32m'  * Render the list of uploaded filenames using `rx.foreach`.\n'[39m +
                [32m"* The Reflex backend endpoint `POST http://localhost:8000/_upload` must accept multipart uploads, persist the file under the upload directory returned by `rx.get_upload_dir()`, and the uploaded filename must appear in the page's dynamic file list after the request succeeds.\n"[39m +
                [32m'* All background processes started during the task must be terminated before the task is reported complete.'[39m

                    [32m'@dataclasses.dataclass(frozen=True)\n'[39m +
                    [32m'class UploadFile(StarletteUploadFile):\n'[39m +

                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    @property\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    def filename(self) -> str | None:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        """Get the name of the uploaded file.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        Returns:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            The name of the uploaded file.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        """\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        return self.name\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    @property\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    def name(self) -> str | None:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        """Get the name of the uploaded file.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        Returns:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            The name of the uploaded file.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        """\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        if self.path:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            return self.path.name\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        return None\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'@dataclasses.dataclass(frozen=True, kw_only=True, slots=True)\n'[39m +
                    [32m'class UploadChunk:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    """A chunk of uploaded file data."""\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    filename: str\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    offset: int\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    content_type: str\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    data: bytes\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'class UploadChunkIterator(AsyncIterator[UploadChunk]):\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    """An async iterator over uploaded file chunks."""\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    __slots__ = (\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        "_chunks",\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        "_closed",\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        "_condition",\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        "_consumer_task",\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        "_error",\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        "_maxsize",\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    )\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    def __init__(self, *, maxsize: int = 8):\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        """Initialize the iterator.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        Args:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            maxsize: Maximum number of chunks to buffer before blocking producers.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        """\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        self._maxsize = maxsize\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        self._chunks: deque[UploadChunk] = deque()\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        self._condition = asyncio.Condition()\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        self._closed = False\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        self._error: Exception | None = None\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        self._consumer_task: asyncio.Future[Any] | None = None\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    def __aiter__(self) -> Self:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        """Return the iterator itself.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        Returns:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            The upload chunk iterator.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        """\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        return self\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    async def __anext__(self) -> UploadChunk:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        """Yield the next available upload chunk.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        Returns:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            The next upload chunk.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        Raises:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            _error: Any error forwarded from the upload producer.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            StopAsyncIteration: When all chunks have been consumed.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        """\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        async with self._condition:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            while not self._chunks and not self._closed:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                await self._condition.wait()\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            if self._chunks:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                chunk = self._chunks.popleft()\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                self._condition.notify_all()\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                return chunk\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            if self._error is not None:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                raise self._error\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            raise StopAsyncIteration\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    def set_consumer_task(self, task: asyncio.Future[Any]) -> None:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        """Track the task consuming this iterator.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        Args:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            task: The background task consuming upload chunks.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        """\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        self._consumer_task = task\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        task.add_done_callback(self._wake_waiters)\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    async def push(self, chunk: UploadChunk) -> None:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        """Push a new chunk into the iterator.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        Args:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            chunk: The chunk to push.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        Raises:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            RuntimeError: If the iterator is already closed or the consumer exited early.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        """\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        async with self._condition:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            while len(self._chunks) >= self._maxsize and not self._closed:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                self._raise_if_consumer_finished()\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                await self._condition.wait()\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            if self._closed:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                msg = "Upload chunk iterator is closed."\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                raise RuntimeError(msg)\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            self._raise_if_consumer_finished()\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            self._chunks.append(chunk)\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            self._condition.notify_all()\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    async def finish(self) -> None:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        """Mark the iterator as complete."""\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        async with self._condition:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            if self._closed:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                return\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            self._closed = True\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            self._condition.notify_all()\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    async def fail(self, error: Exception) -> None:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        """Mark the iterator as failed.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        Args:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            error: The error to raise from the iterator.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        """\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        async with self._condition:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            if self._closed:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                return\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            self._closed = True\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            self._error = error\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            self._condition.notify_all()\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    def _raise_if_consumer_finished(self) -> None:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        """Raise if the consumer task exited before draining the iterator.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        Raises:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            RuntimeError: If the consumer task completed before draining the iterator.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        """\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        if self._consumer_task is None or not self._consumer_task.done():\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            return\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        try:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            task_exc = self._consumer_task.exception()\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        except asyncio.CancelledError as err:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            task_exc = err\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        msg = "Upload handler returned before consuming all upload chunks."\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        if task_exc is not None:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            raise RuntimeError(msg) from task_exc\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        raise RuntimeError(msg)\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    def _wake_waiters(self, task: asyncio.Future[Any]) -> None:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        """Wake any producers or consumers blocked on the iterator condition.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        Args:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            task: The completed consumer task.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        """\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        task.get_loop().create_task(self._notify_waiters())\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    async def _notify_waiters(self) -> None:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        """Notify tasks waiting on the iterator condition."""\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        async with self._condition:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            self._condition.notify_all()\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'@dataclasses.dataclass(kw_only=True, slots=True)\n'[39m +
                    [32m'class _UploadChunkPart:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    """Track the current multipart file part for upload streaming."""\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    content_disposition: bytes | None = None\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    field_name: str = ""\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    filename: str | None = None\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    content_type: str = ""\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    item_headers: list[tuple[bytes, bytes]] = dataclasses.field(default_factory=list)\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    offset: int = 0\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    bytes_emitted: int = 0\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    is_upload_chunk: bool = False\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'@dataclasses.dataclass(kw_only=True, slots=True)\n'[39m +
                    [32m'class _UploadChunkMultipartParser:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    """Streaming multipart parser for streamed upload files."""\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    headers: Headers\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    stream: AsyncGenerator[bytes, None]\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    chunk_iter: UploadChunkIterator\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    _charset: str = ""\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    _current_partial_header_name: bytes = b""\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    _current_partial_header_value: bytes = b""\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    _current_part: _UploadChunkPart = dataclasses.field(\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        default_factory=_UploadChunkPart\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    )\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    _chunks_to_emit: deque[UploadChunk] = dataclasses.field(default_factory=deque)\n'[39m +

                    [32m'    _stream_chunk_count: int = 0\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    def on_part_begin(self) -> None:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        """Reset parser state for a new multipart part."""\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        self._current_part = _UploadChunkPart()\n'[39m +

                    [32m'        """Record streamed chunk data for the current part."""\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        if (\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            not self._current_part.is_upload_chunk\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            or self._current_part.filename is None\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        ):\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            return\n'[39m +

                    [32m'            UploadChunk(\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                filename=self._current_part.filename,\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                offset=self._current_part.offset + self._current_part.bytes_emitted,\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                content_type=self._current_part.content_type,\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                data=message_bytes,\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            )\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        )\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        self._current_part.bytes_emitted += len(message_bytes)\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        self._emitted_chunk_count += 1\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        self._emitted_bytes += len(message_bytes)\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    def on_part_end(self) -> None:\n'[39m +

                    [32m'        ):\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            self._chunks_to_emit.append(\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                UploadChunk(\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    filename=self._current_part.filename,\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    offset=self._current_part.offset,\n'[39m +

                    [32m'            )\n'[39m +

                    [32m'    ``UploadFilesProvider``, which is declared further down in this module.\n'[39m +
                    [32m"    Returns a fresh instance per call so render-cache state can't leak\n"[39m +
                    [32m'    across compile runs via ``copy.deepcopy``.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    Returns:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        A new VarData carrying the upload-context import, hook, and the\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        ``UploadFilesProvider`` app_wrap declaration.\n'[39m +

                    [32m'    return VarData(\n'[39m +

                    [32m'            "const [filesById, setFilesById] = useContext(UploadFilesContext);": None,\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        },\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        app_wraps=((5, UploadFilesProvider.create()),),\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    )\n'[39m +

            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'Before executing the command, please follow these steps:\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'1. Directory Verification:\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - If the command will create new directories or files, first use listFiles to verify the parent directory exists and is the correct location\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - For example, before running "mkdir foo/bar", first use listFiles to check that "foo" exists and is the intended parent directory\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'2. Command Execution:\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Always quote file paths that contain spaces with double quotes (e.g., cd "path with spaces/file.txt")\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Examples of proper quoting:\n'[39m +
            [32m'     - cd "/Users/name/My Documents" (correct)\n'[39m +
            [32m'     - cd /Users/name/My Documents (incorrect - will fail)\n'[39m +
            [32m'     - python "/path/with spaces/script.py" (correct)\n'[39m +
            [32m'     - python /path/with spaces/script.py (incorrect - will fail)\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - After ensuring proper quoting, execute the command.\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Capture the output of the command.\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'Usage notes:\n'[39m +
            [32m'- The command argument is required.\n'[39m +
            [32m'- You can specify an optional timeout in seconds (up to 300s / 5 minutes). If not specified, commands will timeout after 60s (1 minute).\n'[39m +
            [32m'- If the output exceeds 30000 characters, output will be truncated before being returned to you.\n'[39m +
            [32m'- When issuing multiple commands:\n'[39m +
            [32m'  - If the commands are independent and can run in parallel, make multiple executeCommand tool calls in a single message. For example, if you need to run "git status" and "git diff", send a single message with two executeCommand tool calls in parallel.\n'[39m +
            [32m'  - If the commands depend on each other and must run sequentially, use a single executeCommand call with \'&&\' to chain them together (e.g., `git add . && git commit -m "message" && git push`). For instance, if one operation must complete before another starts (like mkdir before cp, Write before executeCommand for git operations, or git add before git commit), run these operations sequentially instead.\n'[39m +
            [32m"  - Use ';' only when you need to run commands sequentially but don't care if earlier commands fail\n"[39m +
            [32m'  - DO NOT use newlines to separate commands (newlines are ok in quoted strings)\n'[39m +
            [32m"- You shall avoid use the markdown code block syntax (backtick, '`') in your command, as it will be interpreted as a command substitution.\n"[39m +
            [32m"- Before using this tool, you must first think about the context provided in <system-reminder> to understand the user's environment and tailor your commands to ensure they are compatible with their system. You must also consider if the command you need to run should be executed in a specific directory rather than the current working directory, and properly set the `cwd` if necessary.\n"[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'# Committing changes with git\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'Only create commits when requested by the user. If unclear, ask first. When the user asks you to create a new git commit, follow these steps carefully:\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'Git Safety Protocol:\n'[39m +
            [32m'- NEVER update the git config\n'[39m +
            [32m"- NEVER run destructive git commands (push --force, reset --hard, checkout ., restore ., clean -f, branch -D) unless the user explicitly requests these actions. Taking unauthorized destructive actions is unhelpful and can result in lost work, so it's best to ONLY run these commands when given direct instructions \n"[39m +
            [32m'- NEVER skip hooks (--no-verify, --no-gpg-sign, etc) unless the user explicitly requests it\n'[39m +
            [32m'- NEVER run force push to main/master, warn the user if they request it\n'[39m +
            [32m'- CRITICAL: Always create NEW commits rather than amending, unless the user explicitly requests a git amend. When a pre-commit hook fails, the commit did NOT happen  — so --amend would modify the PREVIOUS commit, which may result in destroying work or losing previous changes. Instead, after hook failure, fix the issue, re-stage, and create a NEW commit\n'[39m +
            [32m'- When staging files, prefer adding specific files by name rather than using "git add -A" or "git add .", which can accidentally include sensitive files (.env, credentials) or large binaries\n'[39m +
            [32m'- NEVER commit changes unless the user explicitly asks you to. It is VERY IMPORTANT to only commit when explicitly asked, otherwise the user will feel that you are being too proactive\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'1. You can call multiple tools in a single response. When multiple independent pieces of information are requested and all commands are likely to succeed, run multiple tool calls in parallel for optimal performance. run the following bash commands in parallel, each using the executeCommand tool:\n'[39m +
            [32m'  - Run a git status command to see all untracked files. IMPORTANT: Never use the -uall flag as it can cause memory issues on large repos.\n'[39m +
            [32m'  - Run a git diff command to see both staged and unstaged changes that will be committed.\n'[39m +
            [32m"  - Run a git log command to see recent commit messages, so that you can follow this repository's commit message style.\n"[39m +
            [32m'2. Analyze all staged changes (both previously staged and newly added) and draft a commit message:\n'[39m +
            [32m'  - Summarize the nature of the changes (eg. new feature, enhancement to an existing feature, bug fix, refactoring, test, docs, etc.). Ensure the message accurately reflects the changes and their purpose (i.e. "add" means a wholly new feature, "update" means an enhancement to an existing feature, "fix" means a bug fix, etc.).\n'[39m +
            [32m'  - Do not commit files that likely contain secrets (.env, credentials.json, etc). Warn the user if they specifically request to commit those files\n'[39m +
            [32m'  - Draft a concise (1-2 sentences) commit message that focuses on the "why" rather than the "what"\n'[39m +
            [32m'  - Ensure it accurately reflects the changes and their purpose\n'[39m +
            [32m'3. You can call multiple tools in a single response. When multiple independent pieces of information are requested and all commands are likely to succeed, run multiple tool calls in parallel for optimal performance. run the following commands:\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Add relevant untracked files to the staging area.\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Create the commit with a message ending with:\n'[39m +
            [32m'   🤖 Generated with [Pochi](https://getpochi.com) | [Task](<Share URL>)\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'   Co-Authored-By: Pochi <noreply@getpochi.com>\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'   Note: Replace <Share URL> with the Share URL from system information if available. If not available, omit the " | [Task](...)" part.\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Run git status after the commit completes to verify success.\n'[39m +
            [32m'   Note: git status depends on the commit completing, so run it sequentially after the commit.\n'[39m +
            [32m'4. If the commit fails due to pre-commit hook changes, retry the commit ONCE to include these automated changes. If it fails again, it usually means a pre-commit hook is preventing the commit. If the commit succeeds but you notice that files were modified by the pre-commit hook, you MUST amend your commit to include them.\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'Important notes:\n'[39m +
            [32m'- NEVER update the git config\n'[39m +
            [32m'- NEVER run additional commands to read or explore code, besides git bash commands\n'[39m +
            [32m'- DO NOT push to the remote repository unless the user explicitly asks you to do so\n'[39m +
            [32m'- IMPORTANT: Never use git commands with the -i flag (like git rebase -i or git add -i) since they require interactive input which is not supported.\n'[39m +
            [32m'- IMPORTANT: Do not use --no-edit with git rebase commands, as the --no-edit flag is not a valid option for git rebase.\n'[39m +
            [32m'- If there are no changes to commit (i.e., no untracked files and no modifications), do not create an empty commit\n'[39m +
            [32m'- In order to ensure good formatting, ALWAYS pass the commit message via a HEREDOC, a la this example:\n'[39m +
            [32m'<example>\n'[39m +
            [32m`git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'\n`[39m +
            [32m'Commit message here.\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'🤖 Generated with [Pochi](https://getpochi.com) | [Task](<Share URL>)\n'[39m +

            [32m'   - Run a git status command to see all untracked files (never use -uall flag)\n'[39m +

                    [32m'def get_upload_files_context_var_data() -> VarData:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    """Build the VarData for vars reading the upload-files React context.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    Defined as a function (not a module-level value) because it references\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    ``UploadFilesProvider``, which is declared further down in this module.\n'[39m +
                    [32m"    Returns a fresh instance per call so render-cache state can't leak\n"[39m +
                    [32m'    across compile runs via ``copy.deepcopy``.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    Returns:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        A new VarData carrying the upload-context import, hook, and the\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        ``UploadFilesProvider`` app_wrap declaration.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    """\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    return VarData(\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        imports={\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            "react": "useContext",\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            f"$/{Dirs.CONTEXTS_PATH}": "UploadFilesContext",\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        },\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        hooks={\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            "const [filesById, setFilesById] = useContext(UploadFilesContext);": None,\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        },\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        app_wraps=((5, UploadFilesProvider.create()),),\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    )\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'def upload_file(id_: str | Var[str] = DEFAULT_UPLOAD_ID) -> Var:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    """Get the file upload drop trigger.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    This var is passed to the dropzone component to update the file list when a\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    drop occurs.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    Args:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        id_: The id of the upload to get the drop trigger for.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    Returns:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        A var referencing the file upload drop trigger.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    """\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    id_var = LiteralStringVar.create(id_) if not isinstance(id_, Var) else id_\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    var_name = f"""e => setFilesById(filesById => {{\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    const updatedFilesById = Object.assign({{}}, filesById);\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    updatedFilesById[{id_var!s}] = e;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    return updatedFilesById;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'  }})\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    """\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    return Var(\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        _js_expr=var_name,\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        _var_type=EventChain,\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        _var_data=VarData.merge(\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            get_upload_files_context_var_data(), id_var._get_all_var_data()\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        ),\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    )\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'def selected_files(id_: str | Var[str] = DEFAULT_UPLOAD_ID) -> Var:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    """Get the list of selected files.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    Args:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        id_: The id of the upload to get the selected files for.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    Returns:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        A var referencing the list of selected file paths.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    """\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    id_var = LiteralStringVar.create(id_) if not isinstance(id_, Var) else id_\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    return Var(\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        _js_expr=f"(filesById[{id_var!s}] ? filesById[{id_var!s}].map((f) => f.name) : [])",\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        _var_type=list[str],\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        _var_data=VarData.merge(\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            get_upload_files_context_var_data(), id_var._get_all_var_data()\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        ),\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    ).guess_type()\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'@CallableEventSpec\n'[39m +
                    [32m'def clear_selected_files(id_: str = DEFAULT_UPLOAD_ID) -> EventSpec:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    """Clear the list of selected files.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    Args:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        id_: The id of the upload to clear.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    Returns:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        An event spec that clears the list of selected files when triggered.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    """\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    # UploadFilesProvider assigns a special function to clear selected files\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    # into the shared global refs object to make it accessible outside a React\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    # component via `run_script` (otherwise backend could never clear files).\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    return run_script(Var("__clear_selected_files")._as_ref().to(FunctionVar).call(id_))\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'def cancel_upload(upload_id: str) -> EventSpec:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    """Cancel an upload.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    Args:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        upload_id: The id of the upload to cancel.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    Returns:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        An event spec that cancels the upload when triggered.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    """\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    controller = Var(f"__upload_controllers_{upload_id}")._as_ref()\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    return run_script(f"{controller}?.abort()")\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'def get_upload_dir() -> Path:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    """Get the directory where uploaded files are stored.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    Returns:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        The directory where uploaded files are stored.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    """\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    Upload.is_used = True\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    uploaded_files_dir = environment.REFLEX_UPLOADED_FILES_DIR.get()\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    uploaded_files_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    return uploaded_files_dir\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'uploaded_files_url_prefix = Var(\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    _js_expr="getBackendURL(env.UPLOAD)",\n'[39m +

                content: [32m'import reflex as rx\n'[39m +
                  [32m'\n'[39m +
                  [32m'class State(rx.State):\n'[39m +
                  [32m'    uploaded_files: list[str] = []\n'[39m +
                  [32m'\n'[39m +
                  [32m'    async def handle_upload(self, files: list[rx.UploadFile]):\n'[39m +
                  [32m'        for file in files:\n'[39m +
                  [32m"            # Read each uploaded file's bytes asynchronously\n"[39m +
                  [32m'            upload_data = await file.read()\n'[39m +
                  [32m'            # Save the bytes to the Reflex upload directory using the original filename\n'[39m +
                  [32m'            upload_dir = rx.get_upload_dir()\n'[39m +
                  [32m'            upload_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n'[39m +
                  [32m'            file_path = upload_dir / file.filename\n'[39m +
                  [32m'            with open(file_path, "wb") as f:\n'[39m +
                  [32m'                f.write(upload_data)\n'[39m +
                  [32m'            # Append filename to state list\n'[39m +
                  [32m'            self.uploaded_files.append(file.filename)\n'[39m +
                  [32m'\n'[39m +
                  [32m'def index() -> rx.Component:\n'[39m +
                  [32m'    return rx.container(\n'[39m +
                  [32m'        rx.vstack(\n'[39m +

                  [32m'            rx.text("Upload plain text (.txt) and PNG image (.png) files below."),\n'[39m +

                  [32m'                rx.vstack(\n'[39m +
                  [32m'                    rx.button("Select File", color_scheme="blue"),\n'[39m +

                  [32m'                id="upload1",\n'[39m +
                  [32m'                accept={\n'[39m +

                  [32m'                padding="5em",\n'[39m +
                  [32m'            ),\n'[39m +

                  [32m'            rx.vstack(\n'[39m +

            [32m'IMPORTANT: This tool is for terminal operations like git, npm, docker, etc. DO NOT use it for file operations (reading, writing, editing, searching, finding files) - use the specialized tools for this instead.\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'Before executing the command, please follow these steps:\n'[39m +
